title: Online Colleges & Other Questions about my projects - Request

enrichment.summary: Kyle responds to a viewer question about online college, sharing that he personally wouldn't pursue it because he prefers radical self-directed learning, believes degrees are nearly obsolete, and thinks the best way to master a subject is to build the ultimate educational resource on it yourself. He also gives a project update on his history timeline website, saying the explanation page won't be touched until mid-2015 at earliest, and discusses his life strategy of forgoing friends and family to focus on his website and timeline projects.
enrichment.speaker: kyle
topics: life strategy and social isolation by choice

stances.position: wouldn't do it himself; prefers teaching yourself for free; recommends it only if you truly need a credential
already nearly obsolete; will be common-knowledge obsolete within ~20 years as colleges go free and merit-based
functions like bullying — prescribed pace is never in alignment with individual learning style
superior to any formal path; best method is to become the world's best teacher of a subject by building educational content on it
perfect vehicles for becoming a polymath because you can listen to audiobooks while working
still in progress but deprioritized; explanation page and CSS work deferred to mid-2015 at earliest; needs grant funding to work on it full-time
the template for any aspiring future president — a complete expression of one's mind, beliefs, and imagination
deliberately chosen to forgo friends and family in order to focus on his website and timeline projects

entities: Mary Wollstonecraft (author; Kyle is listening to her work)
Yale (cited as example of free online lectures on YouTube)
Google Hangouts (mentioned as under-used debate platform)

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I already went to college, so I wouldn't go back to college.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: My philosophy is to learn things yourself rather than going back to school.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I believe you should be able to get a high-end job without a degree.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I never liked school because the prescribed pace never aligned with my style.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: School feels like bullying to me, pushing people around to fit a certain mold.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: If I hadn't been to school and needed a degree for jobs, I would consider online college for cost and flexibility.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I don't go to college for the social aspect, unlike many people.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I think colleges will become free and more about gathering places than degrees in the future.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: College degrees will become obsolete within about 20 years.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: Online education could be purely meritocratic, unlike traditional grading systems.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: Teachers have given me lower grades than promised multiple times, showing grading is subjective.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: You can learn almost anything online without needing a formal degree, like watching Yale classes on YouTube.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: For hands-on professions like medicine or law, some in-person practice is necessary, but not all education has to be in school.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: There should be online debate platforms where people can practice arguing, like for aspiring lawyers.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I want to be an amateur polymath, learning across many fields while working a labor job.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: Labor jobs are great for becoming a polymath because you can listen to audiobooks while working.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: The smarter you get, the more you can monetize your knowledge through platforms like YouTube.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: The future economy will shift more toward service-related industries like teaching and lecturing.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: People should think twice before pursuing an online degree and consider self-learning instead.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I’m working on my personal website before returning to my timeline project.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I plan to seek educational grants to fund my timeline project so I can work on it full-time.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: Completing my timeline project could take 10 years if I work four hours a day or five years if I work eight hours a day.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I believe my timeline project will change the world by making history more accessible and engaging.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: My personal website is a template for future leaders to share their beliefs and ideas openly.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I’m inventing new ways to design websites using simple HTML and layout philosophy.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: A well-designed website can be like stepping into someone’s imagination, with intricate links and layouts.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I’ve decided to prioritize my work over friendships and relationships to focus on my projects.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I motivate myself to exercise by linking it to improving my website and overall productivity.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I learn best by creating condensed, organized lists of information, which I then share on my website.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: If I want to master a subject, I become the grandmaster teacher of it by creating the ultimate educational resource.
